sheet_name: แบบขยายรั้วแบบที่ 1
source_image: image/บ้าน_เล็ก_1ชั้น_01/บ้าน_เล็ก_1ชั้น_01_หน้า16.png
view_title: แปลน + รูปด้านหน้า + ขยายเสา C1 + ขยายคานคอดิน GB + รูปตัด (รั้วแบบที่ 1)

elements.structure_group: รั้วบ้าน แบบที่ 1 (perimeter fence, NOT the main house structural column schedule)
elements.spec_source: printed on A-14 detail box 'ขยายเสา C1' 1:20
confidence_flags: CONTENT_DISAGREEMENT_vs_t3: the sheet prints only ONE single line '4-ø12 STR:ø6@150' for this column's cross-section (4 corner bars total in a 200x200mm tied column) -- there is no separate top-line/bottom-line the way GB below shows two distinct printed specs. t3 populated main_bar.top.count=4 AND main_bar.bottom.count=4, which if summed via top.count+bottom.count (as the schema doc's own worked example instructs for beams) would double-count to 8 bars, contradicting what is actually drawn (4 total). Kept the same top/bottom structure here only because the schema mandates it be always-nested, but added 'total_main_bar_count':4 as the authoritative field and flag this as a schema-format risk, not a beam-style genuine top/bottom differentiation -- columns do not have a top/bottom rebar split the way beams do; their bars run the full height and are counted once around the perimeter.

elements.structure_group: รั้วบ้าน แบบที่ 1 (perimeter fence ground tie-beam, คานคอดิน — connects the two C1 posts at grade)

additional_bars.position: เสริมดัดโค้งที่ L/5 (bent bar at L/5)
additional_bars.note: printed as '2-ø9+1ø9 BENT L/5' on the GB cross-section detail — read as 2 straight main bars plus 1 additional bent bar, not 3 straight bars

elements.spec_source: printed on A-14 detail box 'ขยายคานคอดิน GB' 1:20
confidence_flags: TOP/BOTTOM_SPLIT_IS_GENUINE_HERE_unlike_C1: the sheet prints TWO distinct spec lines ('2-ø9' and '2-ø9+1ø9 BENT L/5'), consistent with a real beam-style top-vs-bottom differentiation, not a single total being force-split

elements.structure_group: รั้วบ้าน แบบที่ 1 (perimeter fence footing, under each C1 post)
elements.main_bar_note: 3-ø9 (read as 3 bars, 9mm, round/RB per printed Ø symbol) with STR:ø6 @100 ties, shown in the รูปตัด (section) cross-section box under each post
elements.spec_source: printed on A-14 section ('รูปตัด') view, footing box labeled 'F'

elements.description: เสาเข็ม 2-0.15x0.15x4.00ม (2 precast concrete piles per footing, 0.15x0.15m section, 4.00m length), with DOWEL ø12 ยาว 1.00ม connecting the pile to the footing ('เทคอนกรีตยึดกับเสาเข็ม')

elements.material: เหล็ก Ø3/4"x3/4" LG ปลายตัด 45 องศา @ 15 ซม.
elements.description: Vertical square-tube fence pickets between C1 posts, spaced 150mm on center, ends cut at 45 degrees (called out twice on the section view, upper and lower zone, same spec both times)
Maximum post-to-post bay spacing 'ระยะไม่เกิน 3.00 ม.' (post spacing not to exceed 3.00m); column setback from bay edge 0.30m (0.15+0.15 split)

Front-elevation ('รูปด้านหน้า') vertical dimension chain, top to bottom: small 0.10/0.10 bands near the top rail/cap, then a 2.00m overall band further broken into 1.35 (upper picket zone) and 0.90/0.40/0.30 (lower zone incl. GB and footing offsets)

warnings: PATTERN unchanged from t3 ('section') -- this sheet has genuine RC rebar specs (bar counts/diameters/spacing) for column/tie-beam/footing marks, a textbook fit for the new taxonomy's 'section' definition.

This is a garden/perimeter fence structural detail sheet, drawn with its own C1 (column), GB (tie beam) and F (footing) marks. These mark LETTERS are commonly also used for the main house's real structural columns/ground-beams/footings in the S-series structural drawings (outside this extraction's page range) — treat C1/GB/F here as belonging exclusively to 'รั้วบ้าน แบบที่ 1' (fence type 1), not as evidence about the main house's C1/GB/F specs.

CONTENT DISAGREEMENT vs t3: flagged at the C1 element above -- t3's main_bar.top/bottom=4/4 for the column risks a 2x overcount (8 vs the printed 4) if summed per the schema doc's own beam convention. This is a genuine independent-read finding, not a copy of t3, since my own first-pass read of the 'ขยายเสา C1' box also saw only one '4-ø12' line. Added 'total_main_bar_count':4 to disambiguate without removing the schema-mandated top/bottom structure.

All primary rebar counts/diameters/spacings (4-ø12, STR:ø6@150 for C1; 2-ø9+1ø9 BENT L/5, STR:ø6@150 for GB; 3-ø9, STR:ø6@100 for F) are printed clearly and read at high confidence; per rule 5.4 all 'ø' (plain circle) symbols were read as RB (round bar), never inferred as DB from diameter size.

Sibling sheet A-15 (แบบขยายรั้วแบบที่ 2, หน้า17) shows the same C1 column and F footing specs but a different infill (concrete block instead of steel pickets) and a ทับหลัง (lintel) instead of a GB tie beam — see _หน้า17_fence_type2.json.
Nothing here changes or adds to the main building gridline master file (หน้า00_gridline.json) — no named or dummy grid intersections are referenced on this sheet.
